page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv9 architecture, it features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gb.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. Multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang, rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 21,353.49 GB/sec and large block uncached writes reaching 483.46 GB/sec. However, single-core performance remains moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average tier.

Due to its high core count and substantial memory bandwidth, this instance is highly efficient for parallelized workloads. It excels in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, all of which achieved top-tier benchmark results. Conversely, it is less suited for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or specialized hardware acceleration, as it lacks a GPU and shows weaker performance in low-parameter LLM prompt processing. The instance represents a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for enterprise-scale general-purpose applications, high-concurrency web serving, and large-scale database deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by 192 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 768.0 GB of memory (4.0 GB per core), a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, though single-core performance is average. Lacking a GPU, it is not designed for hardware-accelerated tasks. This instance is highly cost-efficient for parallelized, resource-dense workloads, making it an excellent fit for high-concurrency web serving, Redis deployments, and enterprise database management.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is an ARMv9-based general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated cores, 768 GB of RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-concurrency databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is an ARMv9 general-purpose instance with 192 dedicated cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-concurrency, dedicated ARMv9 instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and multi-core performance for enterprise databases and web serving.

Equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database throughput
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware acceleration
